channel.title: Hackathon on Peter's website
channel.description: Recent content in Hackathon on Peter's website

item.title: Reflection of Global Legal Hackathon: LegalTech may bring new career opportunities
item.description: Before joining the competition, I talked with friends in the legal industry and identified the following problems in Hong Kong: The annual PCLL quota hinders the career prospect of many legal degree graduates Staff efficiency is one of the KPIs in a legal firm. The firm should have a strong demand to improve staff efficiency via digital transformation. After 54 hours of hacking, Legot team is honored to receive the second runner up award of the first legal Hackathon in Hong Kong.

item.title: Reflection of AS Watson Hackathon: Datathon is the future
item.description: A standard Hackathon is usually a community-driven competition without a specific goal. A data-centric Hackathon (Datathon), on the other hand, is mainly driven by industry with a goal to drive technical innovation and talent acquisition with a provided dataset and challenges faced by the real business. Rather than sponsoring community-driven Hackathon, I foresee that more and more business will take a lead to organize challenges/competitions, instead of treating Hackathon as a marketing and promotional event.

item.title: Hard to find developers? A 5-minute read for tech recruiting via corporate hackathon!
item.description: Background I am an experienced Hackathon organizer, participant, judge, mentor, and a HealthTech startup co-founder. I am now planning with a number of experienced hackathon organizers to re-invent tech recruiting via corporate hackathon. Feel free to drop me a message if you are interested. The focus of this article is mainly for tech recruiting. If the main purpose is just for branding, sponsoring a hackathon should be the most cost-effective solution.

item.title: JeJu Hackathon at South Korea (Day 2)
item.description: In Day 2, the main focus was the idea development. After some discussion, we agreed to set our goal as an application to help promote small businesses at JeJu. Step 1 - Idea Development As our goal was finalized, we decided to choose a team name which can exactly reflect the goal. We independently wrote names and then tried to incorporate all key ideas from the names. Finally, we combined 2 key ideas: “blog” and “story telling” as our team and application name - Blogory

item.title: JeJu Hackathon at South Korea (Day 1)
item.description: Yes, I travelled to JeJu, South Korea. We couldn’t book a direct flight, and there were serious flight delay, we were forced to sleep at Shanghai airport. After 24 hours of travelling, we finally arrive JeJu, South Korea. Step 1 - On-the-group research for the Hackathon We arrived at 3pm. We immediately started the on-the-ground research because we were extremely hungry. We spotted a very nice restaurant near our competition venue.

item.title: Hackathon in Hong Kong (Sep 2016)
item.description: Background I have long been thinking of writing monthly articles about Hackathon in Hong Kong. First, I can foresee that I can involve at least one Hackathon based in Hong Kong in the next few months. Second, if there is no such Hackathon being organized, I should have enough experience to retrospect some past Hackathons. Last but not least, I never spot any similar article before in Hong Kong. I helped organize Hackathon since 2014 and I really want to spread Hackathon culture locally.
